providers.name: Local Codex Worker
providers.description: Runs on the Mac and can access local repos, simulators, certs, and file paths.

providers.name: Cloud Codex Worker
providers.description: General cloud worker for planning, coding, and document synthesis.

providers.name: Web Research Worker
providers.description: Research-oriented worker for sourcing, market scans, and public-web lead discovery.

roles.name: Coordinator
roles.description: Owns the brief, splits the work, tracks status, and closes the effort.

roles.name: App Worker
roles.description: Implements repo-scoped product changes in the application codebase.

roles.name: Market Researcher
roles.description: Finds segments, competitor patterns, and market signals with citations.

roles.name: Lead Sourcer
roles.description: Builds raw lead lists with provenance and public contact paths.

roles.name: Reviewer
roles.description: Checks completeness, provenance, duplicates, and handoff quality.

goals.name: Useful mobile version of AMS
goals.summary:
Make the UI responsive enough to be able to use the app on mobile.

Also need to be able to access it from mobile phone and actually run tasks and such.
goals.successSignal: I can use the app from my phone, away from my macbook.  And the UI is tolerable enough to actually use at least 1 time a day.

goals.name: Improve marketing funnel of Kwipoo
goals.summary: We need Kwipoo to have more of a public facing presence and funnel. So really, need to actually start marketing Kwipoo.
goals.successSignal: We have at least a trickle, 1-10 users signing up and using the app

goals.name: Reduce friction and frustration with repo management
goals.summary: Doing development locally and keeping in sync with the remote repos and different repos and different projects is not as much of a headache and cluster. Is what I want. There's a lot of clean up I'm relying on AI for to keep the repos and branches clean. And it shouldn't be so sketchy.
goals.successSignal:
The ideation to deployment process doesn't have multiple rounds of back and forth with AI to keep the repo and branches clean. 

10 releases happen without any AI reliant clean up.

goals.name: Win the Inocrowd Animal Welfare Monitoring System contest and the prize
goals.summary: I want to win the contest this Innocrowd organization is having to submit proposals for a system that helps the Seeker improve the overall results of their cattle operations, I believe dairy in particular, by enhancing the capabilities of systemically monitoring the health and behavior of their cattle to maximize welfare and business outcomes.
goals.successSignal: I have officially been notified that I have won the contest.

goals.name: Have a consistent, stable UI
goals.summary: The app needs to have a stable, comprehensible UI that has no glaring problems
goals.successSignal: The UI is not a scattered mess, and navigation and information digestion are are least tolerable.

goals.name: Agent Management System prototype milestone
goals.summary: Build the Agent Management System to the point of a useful prototype and 10x my productivity.
goals.successSignal: I am using the Agent Management System consistently and receiving noticeable benefit from it at least 1 time per work day

goals.name: Kwipoo app + website release sync
goals.summary: Coordinate release-facing content and cross-repo app and website alignment.

goals.name: Kwipoo B2C market research
goals.summary: Define the first ICP and source reachable public communities and creators.

workers.name: Colin Mac Local
workers.note: Best for repo changes that need local files or simulator access.

workers.name: Cloud Coordinator
workers.note: Owns task routing, artifact checks, and final handoff prep.

workers.name: Research Cloud
workers.note: Use for market scans, competitor pulls, and source logs.

tasks.title: Improve UI responsiveness and mobile appearance
tasks.summary: Please begin improving the UI so it can handle better resizing and is more responsive and has a better mobile UX and UI. Start by analyzing the current state and making a plan, based on best practices for responsive design and mobile design.

tasks.title: Sync documentation with current app version
tasks.summary: Please start reviewing current app features and functionality, and updating the documentation and guides that are hosted on the website. Let's start with reviewing introduction and creating an account, make sure those are up to date. or I guess add them if they don't even exist in the documentation. but then after that I think we make sure documentation on Things is up to date

tasks.title: Move to actual SQLite
tasks.summary: Please put together a plan to migrate the app to actually using a flavor or package of sqlite, or whatever is most appropriate, instead of the potentially risky sqlite bundled with node

tasks.title: Improve Thread categorization
tasks.summary: Please improve the categorization of Threads, ideally to help improve the capability of assigning new tasks to existing threads when applicable. And enhancing context, making the discovery of context more efficient.

tasks.title: Improve Task context awareness in Thread detail UI
tasks.summary: Improve Task context awareness in Thread detail UI. As in, make it easier or a bit more prominent in the UI on the Thread detail page to see which Task the thread is currently working on. SO like if you're typing a response or reading the latest response from the thread, it's easier to recognize what you're working on

tasks.title: Review information architecture of app
tasks.summary: Please take a pass over the information architecture of the app, using the information-architecture skill and any other relevant skills, and make a plan to improve the app's handling and architecture of information, particularly around app presentation and navigation

tasks.title: Maintain form input values
tasks.summary: The forms (starting with create task at least) should keep their input values/state between reloads. i.e., I don't want my work to be lost in a form if the page reloads

tasks.title: Format Tasks page content width (retry)
tasks.summary: The content on the Tasks page doesn't take up the full width of the like container, there's space on the right that's empty for no apparent reason and it looks weird. Please have at least the Tasks queue table take up like full width

tasks.title: Add Target Date field to tasks
tasks.summary: Add a Target Date field to Tasks. Use the appropriate field type and all that, obviously.

tasks.title: Remove noise from prototype
tasks.summary: Please review the like existing entities/features (e.g. Projects, Planning, Runs, Goals, etc.) in this app and explain what each is currently doing or being used for, from what you can tell.

tasks.title: RAG knowledge and notes
tasks.summary: Please expand on the Retrieval-Augmented Generation note and related notes in my obsidian vault. Especially add any knowledge or info that would be useful for designing or implemented a RAG engine.

tasks.title: Stabilize repeated failure path for Planning work and chunking
tasks.summary: Investigate the repeated failed or blocked runs for this task. Latest failure detail: The linked work thread failed. Document the root cause and add a concrete safeguard so future runs do not hit the same failure mode.

tasks.title: Planning page and functionality
tasks.summary: Do research and analysis and develop a plan to implement planning functionality, I'm envisioning a sort of interactive way of like setting your planning horizon, determining and slotting goals for the horizon, back planning tasks, subtasks, subgoals, etc. off the goals. Linking/relating the goals to projects, tasks, subgoals, etc. Determining available capacity and that stuff. And ideally, the more advanced version of having like expert Artificial Intelligence assistance in planning and creating tasks and executing well developed adaptable plans.

tasks.title: Self-improving system
tasks.summary: Research and develop a plan to implement functionality for the agent management system to self-improve over time. I.e. set up some like feedback loops and adaptability and knowledge acquisition and refinement and skill development so it can continuously improve itself.

tasks.title: Improve UX of creating and managing goals
tasks.summary: Creating goals is confusing and low value right now. The UX of creating and managing goals is poor, needs improvement. Linking/relating goals/subgoals to each other should be easier. The UX of linking/relating between goals and projects/tasks/goals/etc. is lack luster currently. Needs to be more consistent and intuitive than it currently is. Do appropriate research then develop a plan to improve the Goal functionality for the purposes of this system.

tasks.title: Enhance agent/thread capabilities
tasks.summary: I received a response that you could not complete rendered-browser inspection in the sandbox. If we can, let's add the ability to enable the agents/threads to access stuff like the sandbox so they can perform more effectively.

tasks.title: Plan a UI improvement sweep
tasks.summary: The UI is pretty messy and inconsistent. And not optimized, kinda feels mostly thrown together. Please review, analyze, and research then come up with a plan to improve the UI, like figure out how to best layout the different pages based on their contents and purpose, find the right components and layouts and styles to use from the skeleton UI library and all that. Also need to plan out like good patterns, like having lists/table views to view many like entities and preview info about them, then be able to drill into detail pages for single entites/objects/records whatever. So need to like follow better practices and better adapt the UI for the purpose of the pages and content and such

tasks.title: Goal page and access
tasks.summary: Please start scaffolding a page for creating Goals. Goals are/will be like the high level objectives that work should ultimately progressing towards. Goals can link to projects and tasks. Probably should have sub goals. But essentially, Goals will serve as desirable outcomes, further out goals will probably be more vague, and goals will probably change or become more clear over time. And effectively, it'll be like goals are set then backplanning will be done to create sub goals, projects, tasks, subtasks, etc. But let's start simple.

tasks.title: Fix overflowing text
tasks.summary: Please fix instances like those shown in the attached screenshot where text is horizontally overflowing out of their containers.

tasks.title: Fix Price/cost input field
tasks.summary: The cost field on the thing form won't let you use a decimal, it seems like it's immediately removing the decimal after you type it in. The field needs to be fixed, UI improved. It should function optimally for a field that will have prices, costs, etc. types of data entered into it.

tasks.title: move Task Ideation pane
tasks.summary: Please reduce the prominence of the Task Ideation pane on the Tasks page. It should be like collapsable and let's try below the Tasks queue. This way the Tasks queue table has more space available

tasks.title: Thread status change
tasks.summary: Change the Ready status for a thread to Available. I.e., the thread is available for use. "Ready" is confusing bc it could be interpreted as like it has something ready for the user. Like "the pie is ready".

tasks.title: Accurately reflect task status
tasks.summary: If a task output is responded to, then the task should be like automatically updated back to In Progress. Right now I think it just stays on awaiting review.

tasks.title: Documentation process improvement
tasks.summary:
You are working on the Kwipoo project.

Context:
- There is the app itself and the website.
- Documentation for how to use the app, what features exist, what terms mean, what fields mean, and what different entities/objects contain needs to live on the website.
- The app is still changing fairly quickly, so documentation can become stale easily.
- We want a streamlined, low-friction process for keeping docs accurate as the app evolves and for getting updated documentation deployed to the website.
- There is already some versioning / release infrastructure in place, including versions and some Changesets/release-related setup.
- The app and website live under a shared local project folder.
- The goal is not just “write docs,” but to design and implement a practical system/process that makes docs easier to maintain going forward.

Your task:
Review the current project structure, release/versioning flow, and any existing documentation setup, then design and begin implementing a streamlined documentation maintenance and publishing workflow.

What I want you to do:
1. Inspect the codebase and determine:
   - where the app code lives
   - where the website code lives
   - where documentation currently lives, if anywhere
   - how the website currently consumes or renders documentation
   - what release/versioning/changelog mechanisms already exist
   - whether there is any current CI/CD path that could be extended for docs updates/deployment

2. Based on the current project, propose a practical documentation architecture that is:
   - simple
   - maintainable by a very small team
   - resilient to frequent app changes
   - low-overhead
   - not overengineered

3. Specifically solve for these needs:
   - documenting features, terms, entities, fields, and usage
   - making it easy to update docs when app features change
   - reducing the chance that docs drift out of sync with the app
   - making docs easy to publish to the website
   - fitting docs updates into the existing development/release workflow as naturally as possible

4. Think through whether docs should be:
   - stored in the website repo/app directly
   - stored as markdown/mdsvex/content files
   - partially generated from structured definitions or schemas where appropriate
   - tied to releases/changesets in some way
   - checked by CI for missing updates or stale references

5. Identify where documentation can realistically be sourced from or anchored to, such as:
   - route definitions
   - UI labels
   - forms and field definitions
   - entity/type definitions
   - schemas
   - config files
   - existing copy/content
   - changelogs or release notes

6. Recommend what should be manual vs semi-automated vs automated.
   Be pragmatic. Do not force automation where it adds fragility or complexity.

Deliverables:
- A concise assessment of the current documentation/update/deployment situation
- A proposed workflow for how documentation should be maintained going forward
- A recommended file/folder structure for docs
- A recommendation for how docs should be rendered on the website
- A recommendation for how docs updates should fit into the dev/release workflow
- Any recommended CI checks, scripts, or conventions
- A prioritized implementation plan

Implementation:
After the assessment and proposal, begin implementing the foundation of the recommended approach.
Focus on the minimum useful version first.

That likely means some combination of:
- creating a docs structure
- adding or improving a docs rendering pipeline on the website
- creating reusable docs templates or frontmatter conventions
- adding scripts/checks to support docs maintenance
- adding a lightweight process for tying feature changes to docs updates
- documenting the workflow for the team in a maintainer-facing doc

Important constraints:
- Keep this lightweight and pragmatic for a small fast-moving startup project.
- Avoid enterprise-style process bloat.
- Avoid creating a huge abstract documentation system unless it clearly pays off.
- Prefer explicit, understandable patterns over cleverness.
- Optimize for ease of upkeep, not theoretical elegance.
- Where possible, use what already exists in the project instead of inventing parallel systems.
- Flag any areas where the current codebase structure makes accurate docs harder than it should be.

What I do NOT want:
- a generic explanation of why documentation matters
- a giant speculative redesign of the whole app/site
- heavy process for its own sake
- docs that are disconnected from the actual product structure
- fake automation that looks smart but will be annoying to maintain

What I want instead:
- a grounded review of the current project
- concrete recommendations
- practical implementation
- a maintainable workflow that helps keep docs accurate and easy to publish

Output format:
1. Current-state findings
2. Risks / pain points
3. Recommended documentation architecture
4. Recommended maintenance + publishing workflow
5. Recommended implementation plan
6. Actual changes made
7. Follow-up recommendations

tasks.title: Approve task response/mark task as completed
tasks.summary: User should be able to like approve the task response/output and set/update the task status to complete from the thread detail of the thread that provided the output

tasks.title: add attachments to task from create task form. also, be able to paste in files/attachments into the form as well
tasks.summary: add attachments to task from create task form. also, be able to paste in files/attachments into the form as well

tasks.title: Fix UI of Thread name in other thread table
tasks.summary: The Thread name column is like too wide in the Other threads table on the sessions page. It needs to be narrower so the other columns are visible

tasks.title: status and color standardization
tasks.summary: Standardize and improve the consistency of usage of colors to indicate status. Some of the badges/labels use the same colors for different meanings and it is confusing. And remember, we want to show the user as much as is helpful but not more.

tasks.title: Improve thread status clarity
tasks.summary: The status indicators on thread status are a bit confusing. Like, when it says "awaiting reply" that makes me think the thread is waiting a response from me. So need to be careful with phrasing and such, and want to capture as many states/statuses as necessary/helpful, but not more than that.

tasks.title: Thread naming standardization
tasks.summary: Please implement a more standardized and like clear way of naming sessions/threads. Right now, it's hard to differentiate between them and clearly understand which thread it is.

tasks.title: Categorize work threads
tasks.summary: Please start implementing a functionality where over time, work threads get like categorized based on the domain/topics they are relevant to.

tasks.title: Disable "Run Task" if running
tasks.summary: Users shouldn't be able to "Run" a task again while it is already running. Make sure this is handled appropriately, and is made apparent to the user via the UI

tasks.title: Improve sectioning of Task Detail pa
tasks.summary: The Task Detail page is difficult to process and navigate, please section it better and make the like delineation between sections more clear

tasks.title: Make run and save task buttons more noticeable
tasks.summary: The Run and Save buttons on the task detail are kinda easy to miss. Can you please improve the UI so the buttons are more noticeable.

tasks.title: Create and run
tasks.summary: On the create task form, there should be the option to create and run task. So like next to the Create task button

tasks.title: Assign to relevant available thread
tasks.summary: Start implementing a functionality that suggests a relevant thread that's available to assign a task to. User should still be able to assign the task to a new thread.

tasks.title: Fix Demo profiles task stuck on misaligned directory
tasks.summary: The Demo profiles task in the que is pointed at a directory that doesn't exist anymore so it's like stuck in awaiting review and the thread is stuck being indicated as Starting. so the task needs to be like fixed so it points and the correct location of the Kwipoo project, or like the connection to the thread it can't use anymore must be terminated or disconnected or something

tasks.title: Move create task towards top of Tasks page
tasks.summary: The create task form is somewhat difficult to find, it needs to be somewhere more prominent and easy to access like towards the top of the Tasks page

tasks.title: Ready for approval state
tasks.summary: After the "agent" or thread or whatever finishes the task and is ready for a new message or whatever, the task it was working on should have its status changed to like "needs review" or "needs approval", if we have either of those already. if not create one

tasks.title: Create draft tasks directly from ideation thread output
tasks.summary: Add a workflow that lets the operator review an ideation thread's suggested tasks and create selected ones as draft tasks with project, summary, and default routing prefilled. Reuse the existing ideation thread/session linkage instead of inventing a second ideation store.

tasks.title: Expose full task routing and governance fields in create/edit flows
tasks.summary: Expand task create/edit flows to support priority, risk level, approval mode, requires review, desired role, goal link, blocked reason, and dependency task selection. Keep the quick-create flow lightweight, but make the detail page a complete editor for the task model.

tasks.title: Make runs a first-class surface
tasks.summary: Add a runs index and run detail view with filters for status, task, worker, provider, and time. Show prompt digest, session/thread links, heartbeat age, error summary, and artifact paths so operators can inspect execution outcomes without bouncing between pages.

tasks.title: Surface stale WIP and aging work on home/tasks pages
tasks.summary: Add stale-work indicators based on task updatedAt, last run heartbeat, and thread activity age. Make it visible on the home dashboard and task table, with filters for stale in-progress, no recent run activity, and task has active thread but no recent output.

tasks.title: Add artifact browsing instead of only raw path display
tasks.summary: Add lightweight artifact browsing for task, run, and goal artifact roots, starting with directory listing, file-type badges, and links to known outputs. Keep it read-only at first and reuse existing attachment/download behavior where possible.

tasks.title: Make an assistant that you can run to have it come up with additional useful tasks to create based on the context of your projects and the history of tasks
tasks.summary: Make an assistant that you can run to have it come up with additional useful tasks to create based on the context of your projects and the history of tasks

tasks.title: Do a UI pass to address containers that have text which if the text is long
tasks.summary: Do a UI pass to address containers that have text which if the text is long then it overflows too much or whatever, so like need to elegantly handle varying lengths of text

tasks.title: File attachment for tasks
tasks.summary: Please add the ability to attach files to tasks.

tasks.title: Improve responsiveness
tasks.summary: Improve the responsiveness of the UI, currently for example on the Tasks page if you resize the window, some of the content becomes unviewable unless you widen the window enough

tasks.title: Move project field to top of create task form
tasks.summary: Move project field to top of create task form

tasks.title: Change Running status to In Progress (Tasks)
tasks.summary: Change Running status to In Progress for tasks, also add a "In Draft" status

tasks.title: Reduce size of Thread Continuity in Task detail
tasks.summary: Reduce size of Thread Continuity in Task detail. The Thread preview link cards show too many lines of text if the message is really long. Shouldn't show more than like 5 lines.

tasks.title: Plan for data export
tasks.summary: Please review and analyze for potential options to implement data export for users. Ideally starting out with more basic data export and working up towards a streamline UX for users to export their data. Basically, we want to make it easy for users to take their data with them.

tasks.title: More accurate status indicator on tasks
tasks.summary: Should be able to see if a session/thread is working on a task. make it easy to access the thread from the task detail, and like the task row in the que.

tasks.title: Move adding provider form to button (similar to add project on project page).
tasks.summary: Move adding provider form to button (similar to add project on project page).

tasks.title: Ability to archive threads
tasks.summary: Need the ability to archive threads to like at least filter them out of view in the list of threads in the sessions page. need to be able to multi-select threads and be able to archive multiple at once.

tasks.title: Remove info panels/cards from Providers page
tasks.summary: The info panels/cards feel unnecessary on this page, please remove them

tasks.title: Fix destructive reload
tasks.summary: On some pages and areas of the app, there is intrusive refreshing/reloading to fetch the most recent data. This obviously is poor UX, so need to start fixing the data layer to make it more reactive without interfering with the users activity.

tasks.title: Refine first Kwipoo ICP
tasks.summary: Turn current product language into a tighter B2C ICP with segment fit signals and objections.
